profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-11-03 18:44:11 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Johan Kugelberg hip hop collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The Johan Kugelberg hip hop collection documents the origins of hip-hop as music, culture and community in the Bronx, NY, and its influence on the history of music, art, performance, and activism in America during the final third of the 20th century and beyond. The collection contains nearly 1,000 recordings (primarily LP records), textile art, books and magazines, and more than 500 original party and club flyers designed by Buddy Esquire and others. It also includes 74 candid hip hop photos depicting various music events, record signings and performances (including Raandu Avion, Brandy, Missy Elliott, Mary J. Blige, DMX and Wyclef Jean), as well as memorabilia documenting the 2008 Hip Hop Conference and Celebration at Cornell.

odd.head: General Note
odd.p: Books, LPs, DVDs, and poster associated with the collection are cataloged individually.

odd.head: General Note
odd.p: This collection was previously titled Cornell University Library hip hop collection

odd.head: General Note
odd.p: Joe Conzo Jr. photographs and related material previously in boxes 25-31 have been moved to the Joe Conzo, Jr. photographs and ephemera collection#8091

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Sound recordings on vinyl may be requested and viewed, but not played.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Use of digitized audio visual materials is limited to on-site access only.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Johan Kugelberg hip hop collection, #8021. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Source of acquisition--The founding materials in the collection were the gift of collector and author Johan Kugelberg..

bibliography.head: Publications About Described Materials
bibliography.p: Described in: Born in the Bronx: a visual record of the early days of hip hop / edited by Johan Kugelberg; photographs by Joe Conzo; foreword by Afrika Bambaataa; original flyer art by Buddy Esquire; featuring a timeline by Jeff Chang. New York: Rizzoli, 2007.
